name: Oxygen
description: A limited breathing supply depletes in unsafe volumes such as underwater, vacuum, smoke, or toxic air.
design_notes: Oxygen is a deadline mechanic tied to volumes and recovery sources. Designers need clear breathable_volume_id rules, depletion while swimming or exerting, refill behavior at air pockets or tanks, and warning thresholds that leave time for recovery before drowning or suffocation damage begins.
design_purpose: Add tension to underwater, space, smoke, or hazardous-air exploration.
Make equipment, routes, air pockets, and preparation matter in risky environments.
Create readable short-term pressure without permanently blocking exploration.
player_fantasy: The player feels the urgency of limited breath and the relief of reaching safe air in time.

parameters.name: oxygen_stat_id
parameters.description: Stat identifier used by depletion, refill, warnings, and suffocation effects.

parameters.name: oxygen_remaining_ms
parameters.description: Remaining safe breathing window before depletion effects begin.

parameters.name: breathable_volume_id
parameters.description: Volume or state that refills or preserves oxygen instead of draining it.

parameters.name: oxygen_depletion_per_second
parameters.description: Drain pace while the player is in a non-breathable volume.

parameters.name: refill_source_id
parameters.description: Source that restores oxygen or extends the breathing window.

parameters.name: suffocation_effect_id
parameters.description: Penalty applied after the oxygen window reaches depletion.

edge_cases: The player crosses the waterline repeatedly and the oxygen UI flickers between drain and refill states.
A vehicle, base, or cave counts as breathable while the surrounding volume remains hazardous.
A cutscene, dialogue, or inventory menu opens while the player is already running out of oxygen.
Client prediction refills oxygen at the surface but the server still considers the player submerged.
common_bugs: Oxygen drains while the player is paused or in loading screens because the unsafe volume state remains active.
Refill logic runs every frame and repeatedly plays warning-clear audio or particles.
Drowning damage begins before the zero-oxygen warning animation has completed.
Save/load restores oxygen points but not whether the current volume is breathable.
balancing_notes: Short oxygen windows create strong tension but need nearby readable recovery options.
Equipment upgrades can extend exploration range without removing the need to plan routes.
Damage after depletion should usually tick with a buffer rather than killing instantly unless the genre demands harsh failure.
accessibility_notes: Use numeric countdown, icon state, audio, and optional haptics for oxygen warnings.
Avoid relying only on muffled audio or vignette effects, and offer reduced panic effects for sensitive players.

implementation_notes.unity: Track oxygen from volume enter and exit events plus a survival stat record. Store oxygen_remaining_ms, active breathable_volume_id, and suffocation_effect_id in save data, and let the server validate refill sources in multiplayer.
implementation_notes.godot: Area2D or Area3D nodes can mark breathable and hazardous volumes, but oxygen state should live on the player survival service. Use threshold crossing events for UI warnings so rapid waterline toggles do not spam cues.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Physics Volumes, overlap components, or gameplay tags to determine breathing state. A replicated component should own depletion, refill, and suffocation damage, while clients display predicted warnings from server-corrected oxygen state.
implementation_notes.web: Keep oxygen volume checks in the fixed simulation step and debounce waterline transitions. Persist active volume id and remaining milliseconds so browser reloads do not reset a dangerous dive.

relationships.reason: Oxygen pressure is most readable when it is tied to underwater movement and resurfacing transitions.
Oxygen depletion can convert into health damage once the breath reserve is exhausted.
Breath reserves need visible remaining-capacity feedback before drowning damage begins.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses oxygen supply to define underwater exploration range and risk.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses an air meter when the player remains underwater.
